questions.question:
Consider the following minterm expression for F(P,Q,R,S):
F(P,Q,R,S) = sum(0, 2, 5, 7, 8, 10, 13, 15)
The minterms 2, 7, 8 and 13 are 'do not care' terms. The minimal sum-of-products form for F is
A. Q'S' + QS
B. Q'S' + QS + PQ'S + P'QS'
C. Q'S' + QRS + QR'S'
D. PR + RS + PS
questions.to_find: Minimal SOP expression for F using Karnaugh map with given don't cares

understand.plain: We have a 4-variable Boolean function F with minterms at positions 0,2,5,7,8,10,13,15. Minterms 2,7,8,13 are don't-care terms (we can treat them as 0 or 1 to get the simplest expression). Using a K-map we find the largest possible groupings.

keywords.explain: A minterm whose output can be freely chosen as 0 or 1 to maximise K-map groupings.
keywords.example: Minterm 2 is don't care, so we can include it in any K-map group that helps simplification.

keywords.explain: A largest possible group of 1s (and don't cares) in the K-map that yields a product term.
keywords.example: Group {0,2,8,10} using don't cares yields the prime implicant Q'S'.

understand.visual_alt: 4-variable K-map for F(P,Q,R,S). Blue cells form the Q'S' group {m0,m2,m8,m10} using don't cares. Green cells form the QS group {m5,m7,m13,m15} using don't cares. Together they cover all required minterms.

given.aim: Find the minimal SOP for F using K-map groupings that include don't care terms.

terms.meaning: The ON-set: positions 0,5,10,15 must evaluate to 1 (2,7,8,13 are don't cares).
terms.connects: These positions determine which K-map cells must be covered.

terms.meaning: P=0/1, Q=0, R=0/1, S=0 → common literals Q=0 and S=0 → PI = Q'S'.
R=0/1, S=1, Q=1 across all P → common literals Q=1 and S=1 → PI = QS.

given.plan: Draw the K-map. Use don't cares 2,7,8,13 to form the largest possible groups. Two groups of size 4 emerge: Q'S' and QS, covering all real minterms.

steps.title: Fill K-map and identify groups
steps.apply: Place 1s at real minterms {0,5,10,15}, DC at {2,7,8,13}, 0s elsewhere. Group 1: {m0,m2,m8,m10} – all have Q=0,S=0 → PI = Q'S' (size 4). Group 2: {m5,m7,m13,m15} – all have Q=1,S=1 → PI = QS (size 4).
steps.note: Both groups of size 4 are prime implicants; together they cover every real minterm.

steps.title: Verify coverage and form minimal SOP
steps.apply: Real minterms: m0 ∈ Q'S' ✓, m5 ∈ QS ✓, m10 ∈ Q'S' ✓, m15 ∈ QS ✓. No real minterm is missed. The expression uses only 4 literals.
steps.note: Note that Q'S' + QS is equivalent to Q XNOR S (Q ↔ S), confirming correctness.

solution.result: The minimal SOP is Q'S' + QS (Option A).

questions.formula_note: Karnaugh map simplification with don't-care entries; grouping rule: groups must be powers of 2 and use Gray-code adjacency.

questions.question:
Consider the minterm list form of a Boolean function given below.
F(P, Q, R, S) = sum(0, 2, 5, 7, 9, 11) + d(3, 8, 10, 12, 14)
Here, sum(.) denotes a minterm and d(.) denotes a don't care term. The number of essential prime implicants of the function F is ___
questions.to_find: Number of essential prime implicants of F(P,Q,R,S)

understand.plain: A prime implicant is essential if it is the only prime implicant that covers some minterm. We draw the K-map with the given minterms and don't cares, identify all prime implicants, then check which ones exclusively cover at least one minterm.

keywords.explain: A prime implicant that covers at least one minterm not covered by any other prime implicant; it must appear in every minimal cover.
keywords.example: If only one PI covers m5, that PI is essential.

keywords.explain: A minterm that can be treated as 0 or 1; used to enlarge K-map groups but does not require coverage on its own.
keywords.example: m3 is don't care, so we can include it in a group to make a larger PI without being forced to.

understand.visual_alt: K-map for F(P,Q,R,S) with minterms {0,2,5,7,9,11} and don't cares {3,8,10,12,14}. Three essential prime implicants identified: Q'S' (blue group), P'QS (green, top middle), PQ'S (green, bottom middle).

given.aim: Count essential prime implicants of F(P,Q,R,S) = sum(0,2,5,7,9,11) + d(3,8,10,12,14).

terms.meaning: Group {m0,m2,m8,m10} (using DCs 8,10): Q=0, S=0 across all P,R values.
terms.connects: Covers real minterms m0 and m2.
m5 is only in P'QS and m9 is only in PQ'S, making both essential.

given.plan: Fill the K-map. Find all prime implicants (largest groups). For each real minterm, check if it is covered by exactly one PI — that PI is essential. Count distinct essential PIs.

steps.title: Identify all prime implicants from K-map
steps.apply:
PI-1: Q'S' — group {m0,m2,m8,m10} size 4 (uses DCs m8,m10).
PI-2: P'QS — group {m5,m7} size 2 (m7 is a real minterm).
PI-3: PQ'S — group {m9,m11} size 2.
PI-4: Q'R — group {m2,m3,m10,m11} size 4 (uses DC m3, real m2,m11).
steps.note: PI-4 (Q'R) is a valid prime implicant but covers minterms also covered by other PIs.

steps.title: Determine essential PIs by checking unique coverage
steps.apply:
m0: only in Q'S' → Q'S' is ESSENTIAL.
m5: only in P'QS → P'QS is ESSENTIAL.
m9: only in PQ'S → PQ'S is ESSENTIAL.
m2: in both Q'S' and Q'R — not a uniqueness witness.
m7: only in P'QS (already essential).
m11: in both PQ'S and Q'R — not a uniqueness witness.
steps.note: Q'R is NOT essential because every minterm it covers (m2, m11) is also covered by another PI.

solution.result: Number of essential prime implicants = 3 (Q'S', P'QS, PQ'S).

questions.formula_note: Essential Prime Implicant theorem: an EPI is identified by finding a minterm covered by exactly one prime implicant in the prime implicant table.

questions.question:
Consider a Boolean function f(w, x, y, z) such that
f(w, 0, 0, z) = 1
f(0, z, 1, z) = z
f(1, 1, y, z) = w + y
The number of literals in the minimal sum-of-products expression of f is _________
questions.to_find: Number of literals in the minimal SOP of f(w,x,y,z) derived from partial specifications

understand.plain: The function f is described by three partial rules instead of a full truth table. We use each rule to assign definite 0 or 1 values to specific minterms (the rest become don't cares). Then we build the K-map and find the minimal SOP.

keywords.explain: The function is described only for certain input combinations; remaining inputs are don't cares.
keywords.example: f(w,0,0,z)=1 means all four minterms with x=0,y=0 are forced to 1.

keywords.explain: Total count of variable appearances (complemented or uncomplemented) across all product terms of the minimal SOP.
keywords.example: w + w'y' + yz has 1+2+2 = 5 literals; w + y' + yz has 1+1+2 = 4 literals.

class='lbl ink'>1</text><rect x='244' y='142' width='45' height='38' class='hi'/><text x='262' y='168' class='lbl ink'>1</text><text x='50' y='208' class='sub ink'>10</text><rect x='100' y='182' y='182' width='45' height='38' class='bg'/><text x='206' y='208' class='sub mut'>d</text><rect x='244' y='182' width='45' height='38' class='bg'/><text x='254' y='208' class='sub mut'>d</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: K-map of f(w,x,y,z). Row wx=11 is all 1s. Row wx=10 has 1s in first two columns and DCs in last two. Row wx=00 has 1s in first two columns. One isolated 1 at (01,11)=m7. Three groups identified: w (row 11 and 10), y' (all y=0 cells), yz (m7 and m15).

given.aim: Determine f's full truth table from partial rules, then minimise with K-map to count literals.

terms.meaning: For x=0 and y=0, f=1 regardless of w and z. Fixes m0,m1,m8,m9 = 1.
terms.connects: Creates four known-1 entries in the K-map.

terms.meaning: For w=0 and y=1, and x=z (both same value): f equals that value. So f(0,0,1,0)=0, f(0,1,1,1)=1.
When w=1 and x=1, w=1 so w+y=1 always. Fixes m12,m13,m14,m15 = 1.
terms.connects: Entire wx=11 row of K-map is 1.

given.plan: Assign definite 0/1 to all minterms specified by the three rules; mark remaining cells as don't cares. Apply K-map minimisation aiming for largest groups. Count total literals in the chosen cover.

steps.title: Build truth table from the three rules
steps.apply: Known 1s: m0,m1 (rule1, w=0,x=0,y=0), m8,m9 (rule1, w=1,x=0,y=0), m12,m13,m14,m15 (rule3, w=1,x=1). m7 (rule2, f(0,1,1,1)=1). Known 0: m2 (rule2, f(0,0,1,0)=0). Don't cares: m3,m4,m5,m6,m10,m11 (not specified by any rule).
steps.note: Nine definite 1s, one definite 0 (m2), six don't cares.

steps.title: K-map minimisation — select covering groups
steps.apply:
Group 1 — w: all 8 cells with w=1 (m8,m9,m10(DC),m11(DC),m12,m13,m14,m15). Covers real 1s m8,m9,m12,m13,m14,m15. Expression: w (1 literal).
Group 2 — y': all cells with y=0 (m0,m1,m4(DC),m5(DC),m8,m9,m12,m13). Covers real 1s m0,m1. Expression: y' (1 literal).
Group 3 — yz: cells with y=1,z=1 (m3(DC),m7,m11(DC),m15). Covers real 1 m7. Expression: yz (2 literals).
All real 1s covered. m2 (the only 0): y'=1 when y=0, so m2 (y=1) is NOT covered by y'. w=0 at m2, yz: y=1,z=0 at m2 so not covered. ✓

solution.result: The minimal SOP is w + y' + yz with 4 literals.

questions.formula_note: Karnaugh map with don't-care entries; the partial function specification translates to ON-set, OFF-set, and DC-set; minimal SOP literal count minimised by choosing largest K-map groups.

questions.question:
Consider a Boolean expression given by F(X, Y, Z) = B(0, 3, 5, 6, 7).
Which of the following statements is/are CORRECT?
A. F(X, Y, Z) = B(0, 1, 2, 4) refers to the complement F'
B. The minimal SOP of F contains the term XZ
C. F is independent of input X
D. F is independent of input Z
questions.to_find: Which statement(s) about F(X,Y,Z) = B(0,3,5,6,7) are correct

understand.plain: F is a 3-variable Boolean function with 1s at minterms 0,3,5,6,7. We check each option: whether F has a known complement, whether a specific product term appears in the minimal SOP, and whether F is independent of any variable.

keywords.explain: A product term that is 1 for exactly one input combination.
keywords.example: m5 = X·Y'·Z (binary 101) is 1 only when X=1,Y=0,Z=1.

keywords.explain: F is independent of variable V if changing V never changes the output: F(V=0)=F(V=1) for all other input combinations.
keywords.example: If F=Y then F is independent of X and Z but not Y.

x='50' y='212' class='sub mut'>10  (X=1,Y=0)</text><rect x='130' y='188' width='65' height='28' class='bg'/><text x='155' y='208' class='sub mut'>0</text><rect x='210' y='188' width='65' height='28' style='fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;'/><text x='235' y='208' class='lbl' style='fill:#fff;'>1</text></svg>

given.aim: Determine which statements about F(X,Y,Z) = B(0,3,5,6,7) are correct.

terms.meaning: F=1 at minterms {0,3,5,6,7}; F=0 at minterms {1,2,4}.
terms.connects: Directly used to verify each option.

terms.meaning: F'=1 exactly where F=0, i.e., minterms {1,2,4}.

terms.example: m5=101 and m7=111: X=1,Z=1 in both → term XZ.
terms.connects: Identifies which product terms appear in the minimal SOP.

given.plan: Build truth table for F. For each option: compute complement for A; find minimal SOP terms for B; check variable independence for C and D.

steps.title: Check options C and D: variable independence
steps.formula_raw: F independent of V iff F(V=0) = F(V=1) for all other inputs
steps.apply:
Independent of X? Check (Y=0,Z=0): F(X=0,Y=0,Z=0)=1 (m0), F(X=1,Y=0,Z=0)=0 (m4). Different → NOT independent of X. Option C is WRONG.
Independent of Z? Check (X=0,Y=0): F(0,0,0)=1 (m0), F(0,0,1)=0 (m1). Different → NOT independent of Z. Option D is WRONG.
steps.note: Both independence claims fail with counterexamples.

steps.title: Check option A and find minimal SOP for option B
steps.apply:
Option A: F'=B(1,2,4) [NOT B(0,1,2,4) which has 4 minterms]. So option A as stated is WRONG.
Minimal SOP from K-map: groups {m6,m7}=XY (size 2), {m5,m7}=XZ (size 2), {m3,m7}=YZ (size 2), and m0 isolated → X'Y'Z'.
F = X'Y'Z' + XY + XZ + YZ.
Option B states F contains the term XZ → TRUE (XZ is one of the prime implicants covering m5 and m7).
steps.note: XZ is an essential prime implicant because m5 is only covered by XZ.

solution.result: Only option B is correct: the minimal SOP of F contains the term XZ.

questions.formula_note: 3-variable K-map minimisation; essential prime implicants identified by unique minterm coverage; variable independence check requires finding a counterexample pair.

questions.question:
Consider the following four variable Boolean function in sum-of-product form
F(b_3, b_2, b_1, b_0) = sum(0, 2, 4, 8, 10, 11, 12)
where the value of the function is computed by considering b_3 b_2 b_1 b_0 as a 4-bit binary number, where b_3 denotes the most significant bit and b_0 denotes the least significant bit. Note that there are no don't care terms. Which ONE of the following options is the CORRECT minimal sum-of-products expression for F?
A. b_3'b_2 + b_3b_2' + b_2'b_0'
B. b_1'b_0' + b_2'b_0' + b_3b_2'b_1
C. b_3b_2 + b_3'b_2' + b_3b_2'b_1
D. b_1'b_0' + b_3b_2'b_0 + b_2'b_3'
questions.to_find: Minimal SOP expression for F(b_3,b_2,b_1,b_0) = sum(0,2,4,8,10,11,12)

understand.plain: We have a 4-variable Boolean function with minterms at positions 0,2,4,8,10,11,12. Using a 4-variable K-map we identify the largest possible groups of 1s to form the minimal SOP with fewest literals.

keywords.explain: A 4×4 grid where rows correspond to b_3b_2 (Gray code: 00,01,11,10) and columns to b_1b_0 (Gray code: 00,01,11,10). Adjacent cells differ in exactly one variable.
keywords.example: Cells (row 00, col 00) and (row 10, col 00) are adjacent since b_3b_2 changes from 00 to 10, which differ in only b_3.

keywords.explain: Each minterm must be covered by at least one prime implicant in the final SOP.
keywords.example: m11=1011 is only in the group b_3b_2'b_1, making that PI essential.

understand.visual_alt: K-map for F(b3,b2,b1,b0) with 1s at minterms {0,2,4,8,10,11,12}. The leftmost column (b1b0=00) is all 1s giving b1'b0'. Cells {m0,m2,m8,m10} form group b2'b0'. Cells {m10,m11} form group b3b2'b1.

given.aim: Find the minimal SOP of F = sum(0,2,4,8,10,11,12) with no don't cares.

terms.meaning: All four rows with b1=0,b0=0 are 1. PI = b1'b0' (2 literals).
terms.connects: Covers minterms 0,4,12,8. Essential because m4 and m12 are only here.

terms.meaning: All combinations with b2=0 and b0=0. PI = b2'b0' (2 literals).
Two adjacent cells with b3=1,b2=0,b1=1 give PI = b3b2'b1 (3 literals).
terms.connects: m11 is only covered here → essential.

given.plan: Fill the 4-variable K-map. Identify the three prime implicants. Verify each is essential (covers at least one minterm not covered by the others). Form minimal SOP.

steps.title: Fill K-map and identify prime implicants
steps.note: No larger group can include m11 or m2 without including 0-cells.

steps.title: Verify all PIs are essential and check coverage
steps.apply:
m4: only in b1'b0' → essential. m12: only in b1'b0' → essential.
m2: only in b2'b0' → essential.
m11: only in b3b2'b1 → essential.
Coverage check: m0(b1'b0',b2'b0')✓, m2(b2'b0')✓, m4(b1'b0')✓, m8(b1'b0',b2'b0')✓, m10(b2'b0',b3b2'b1)✓, m11(b3b2'b1)✓, m12(b1'b0')✓. All 7 minterms covered.
steps.note: Total literals: 2+2+3 = 7. All three prime implicants are essential; no reduction is possible.

solution.result: Minimal SOP = b1'b0' + b2'b0' + b3b2'b1 (Option B).

questions.formula_note: 4-variable Karnaugh map; essential prime implicant selection; groups of size 4 (2 literals) and size 2 (3 literals) identified using Gray-code adjacency.
